title: Fresh German Butter Beans Stew
description: A sour and dense German dish featuring butter beans, perfect for a date night, with an al-dente finish and ovo-vegetarian friendly ingredients, cooked in a cazuela.
instructions: Heat the cazuela over medium heat and add the chopped onion to saute until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the fresh butter beans, apple cider vinegar, and vegetable broth to the cazuela.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the butter beans are al-dente.
Stir in the sour cream and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until heated through.
Crack the egg into the cazuela and stir gently to combine, cooking for an additional 2 minutes, until the egg is cooked to desired doneness.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the Fresh German Butter Beans Stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
